verses.text: David cries to the LORD, asking that his prayer be 'set forth before thee as incense' and his lifted hands 'as the evening sacrifice.' He longs for prayer to rise acceptably. The believer's evening petition is sweet to God as the appointed offering.

David cries to the LORD, asking that his prayer be 'set forth before thee as incense' and his lifted hands 'as the evening sacrifice.' He longs for prayer to rise acceptably. The believer's evening petition is sweet to God as the appointed offering.

'Set a watch, O LORD, before my mouth,' he prays, that his heart not incline to evil deeds or the dainties of the wicked. He guards lips and heart. We need God's keeping to be saved from our own tongues and appetites.

'Set a watch, O LORD, before my mouth,' he prays, that his heart not incline to evil deeds or the dainties of the wicked. He guards lips and heart. We need God's keeping to be saved from our own tongues and appetites.

He welcomes a righteous man's rebuke as 'a kindness,' an excellent oil, while still praying amid the calamities of the wicked. He prizes faithful correction. The humble receive a friend's reproof as a healing, not a wound.

He welcomes a righteous man's rebuke as 'a kindness,' an excellent oil, while still praying amid the calamities of the wicked. He prizes faithful correction. The humble receive a friend's reproof as a healing, not a wound.

He welcomes a righteous man's rebuke as 'a kindness,' an excellent oil, while still praying amid the calamities of the wicked. He prizes faithful correction. The humble receive a friend's reproof as a healing, not a wound.

His eyes are fixed on the LORD; he asks to be kept from the traps laid for him, that the wicked fall into their own nets while he escapes. He looks to God for safety. The snares set for the godly often catch their makers instead.
